title: Software Engineer, Applied AI (all levels)
company_focus: AI-powered wealth management platform for financial advisors
description:
About Savvy Wealth:

Wealth management is a $545 billion industry in the US, yet
remains archaic and inefficient with low technology
penetration. 75% of financial advisors don’t offer digital
communication beyond email, and 62% still build financial
plans manually in Excel. This leads to a poor client
experience and results in financial advisors spending over
70% of their time on non-client facing, manual work.

Savvy is changing that. We’re building the most advisor-
centric platform in wealth management: a digital-first
solution that modernizes human financial advice. Advisors
who partner with Savvy tap into AI-powered software,
automated sales and marketing, and seamless back office
workflows to scale faster and spend more time with clients.

We’ve raised over $105M to date from Thrive Capital, Index
Ventures, Canvas Ventures, Mark Casady (former LPL Financial
CEO), and other top-tier investors. Our team is made up of
repeat founders and operators who’ve helped build Airbnb,
Square, Brex, Carta, Facebook, $200B+ RIAs, and more.

Savvy is at a pivotal point in its growth trajectory, having
established strong product-market fit in providing a modern
platform to financial advisors. We’ve surpassed $2.2 billion
in AUM in less than three years, grown 600%+ in the last 18
months, and are entering the next phase of the company which
involves rapid expansion of our product offering and
continued revenue growth. Come help us scale!

Responsibilities

- Build AI-powered product features for advisors and Savvy
  operations teams (e.g., copilots, skilled agents,
  automations, summarization, routing, extraction,
  recommendations), with a bias toward shipping quickly
- Rapidly prototype and deliver using modern AI tooling (e.g.,
  Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, Devin, etc.) while
  maintaining high engineering standards - from using
  individual tools to get work done all the way up to
  leveraging a complex orchestration layer to string
  together a dozen tools to get things done even faster
- Own the end-to-end product development lifecycle - identify
  opportunities, design solutions, ship to production, and
  iterate
- Produce production-grade, readable, maintainable code with
  good testing discipline
- Partner tightly with Product, Design, Data, and business
  stakeholders to define scope, de-risk execution, and
  deliver high-impact outcomes
- Work directly with our customers (financial advisors) to
  learn their workflows and needs directly from the source
- Run fast!

Level-specific expectationsMid-level

- Implement AI workflows using established patterns
  (prompting, tool/function calling, retrieval, evals) and
  improve over time
- Ship well-scoped features independently; break ambiguous
  problems into concrete tasks
- Contribute to code quality via reviews, tests, and
  documentation across your team

Senior

- Build Savvy AI systems (RAG, classification/extraction
  pipelines, agentic workflows)
- Establish team patterns for rapid AI development
  (prompt/versioning strats, eval harnesses (ours and/or
  with vendors), rollouts, observability, testing)
- Lead projects (medium to large) end-to-end
- Mentor others on best practices as they emerge

Staff

- Set technical direction for applied AI across multiple
  product areas
- Drive roadmap shaping with
  Engineering/Product/Design/Leadership (including CxO
  leadership)
- Define platform primitives for AI development (shared
  tooling, libraries, evaluation frameworks, guardrails,
  cost/perf opts)
- Raise the bar org-wide, even beyond the reach of engineering

Must have:

- You’re in AI tools day and night, tinkering on work projects
  as well as personal
- You've built scaling web applications with obvious business
  impact
- System design expertise as it applies to emerging AI
  software development trends, for example, system designs
  that maximize the use of context windows with strong
  separation between components
- Full-stack proficiency (or evidence you can cover your gaps
  via AI tools)
  - We use Rails; React; Next, Postgres, GraphQL
- Proven collaboration abilities in cross-functional startup
  environments with mentoring experience and strong
  technical communication skills

Nice to have:

- Fintech domain knowledge including financial services,
  wealth management, regulatory compliance, or financial
  data integrations
- Modern development ecosystem experience with macOS
  toolchains, CI/CD pipelines, IaaS providers, and
  observability platforms (eg, Datadog)

Benefits:

- Competitive salary and equity package
- Unlimited PTO + paid company holidays
- Access to holistic medical, dental, and vision plans
- Company 401(k), Commuter, and HSA/FSA plans
- NYC office in the heart of Manhattan
- Lunch and snacks provided in the office
- Access to virtual mental health care (Spring Health), vision
  related benefits (XP Health), and health concierge
  (Rightway) to help you find the right care
- Access to counseling for stress management, dependent care,
  nutrition, fitness, legal, and financial issues (Guardian
  WorkLifeMatters EAP)
responsibilities: Build AI-powered product features for advisors and operations teams including copilots, skilled agents, automations, summarization, routing, extraction, and recommendations
Rapidly prototype and deliver using modern AI tooling with complex orchestration layers to string together multiple tools
Own the end-to-end product development lifecycle from opportunity identification through production shipping and iteration
Build AI systems including RAG, classification/extraction pipelines, and agentic workflows
Establish team patterns for rapid AI development including prompt/versioning strategies, evaluation harnesses, rollouts, observability, and testing
Partner with Product, Design, Data, and business stakeholders to define scope, de-risk execution, and deliver high-impact outcomes
Work directly with financial advisors to learn their workflows and needs
use_cases: AI copilots for financial advisors to automate manual workflows
Document summarization and extraction for financial plans and client communications
Intelligent routing systems to direct client inquiries and tasks
RAG-based systems for retrieving relevant financial information and advice
Agentic workflows that automate multi-step financial advisor tasks
Sales and marketing automation powered by AI to help advisors scale
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is an Applied AI role building AI-powered product
features including copilots, skilled agents, RAG systems,
classification/extraction pipelines, and agentic workflows.
The engineer directly implements AI systems using prompting,
tool/function calling, retrieval, and orchestrates complex
layers of AI tools - not just supporting infrastructure.
